detail: Coefficient of variation 0.0114 (unusually tight) with 100% of days favorable, averaging -2.10% vs standard. Under the project's documented 1.2% CV heuristic, this combination warrants tracing recorded prices to vendor invoices before the variance is relied upon.

Day-to-day standard deviation of recorded labor minutes (0.300) is 60% of the median spread across the four dishes (0.497). Under the project's cross-dish heuristic, the difference warrants validating the labor-time capture method; the calculation alone does not identify the cause.

Average variance per order moved from $-0.118 during days 1-23 to $-0.306 during days 24-26, a shift of $-0.189 per order. In this synthetic scenario, no corresponding change in staffing or ingredient sourcing was provided, so the movement warrants follow-up rather than a conclusion about intent.
